stories.headline: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
stories.summary: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

stories.headline: U.S. to Withdraw 5,000 Troops From Germany, Pentagon Says
stories.summary: Officials announced the decision after President Trump expressed annoyance with the German chancellor’s remarks about the Iran war.

stories.headline: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
stories.summary: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

stories.headline: NATO assessing details of US troop withdrawal from Germany
stories.summary: US says it plans to pull about 5,000 troops out of Germany over next six to 12 months amid feud with European allies.

stories.headline: Iran war: What’s happening on day 64 as Trump rejects Tehran’s proposal
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says the latest Iranian peace proposal includes demands he 'can’t agree to'.

stories.headline: US to cut troop levels in Germany by 5,000 amid Trump spat with Merz
stories.summary: The decision to reduce the US deployment to Germany comes amid a row between the two allies over Iran.

stories.headline: US threatens shipping firms with sanctions if they pay Iran tolls
stories.summary: The warning comes as President Donald Trump said he was "not excited" by Iran's latest proposal for a peace deal.

stories.headline: Trump claims other presidents flouted war powers law. It's a mixed record
stories.summary: Both Bushes and Reagan won authorisation for wars, but Obama and Clinton ducked the requirement.

stories.headline: Berkshire cash pile climbs to $380bn as stock sales streak hits 14th quarter
stories.summary: Greg Abel to address shareholders at first gathering since he succeeded Warren Buffett

stories.headline: Spirit Airlines winds down operations after bailout talks founder

stories.headline: Hungary Warns of Drought Hit to Agriculture From April Dry Spell
stories.summary: Hungary’s incoming government said it plans to take emergency steps to help farmers as a dry spell during much of April is leading to a severe drought in the grain-producing country.

stories.headline: Spirit Shuts Operations After White House Bailout Collapses
stories.summary: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. is winding down operations after the troubled US discount carrier buckled under the weight of surging fuel prices and a government bailout dangled by US President Donald Trump fell through.

stories.headline: Pentagon says it will downsize U.S. military presence in Germany
stories.summary: NATO said it was working to understand details of the plan to draw down about 5,000 troops, which coincides with a feud between the president and the German chancellor.

stories.headline: Israeli air strikes kill 10 people in southern Lebanon
stories.summary: Drones and planes hit several towns despite 'ceasefire' since April 17 as Hezbollah pledges to continue its attacks.

stories.headline: Flotilla activists return to Istanbul after Israel detains them at sea
stories.summary: Global Sumud Flotilla activists have arrived in Istanbul after Israel intercepted their Gaza-bound aid convoy at sea.

stories.headline: Spirit Airlines begins ‘wind-down’, cancels all flights over fuel crisis
stories.summary: The collapse of the US-based budget carrier due to a doubling in jet fuel prices will cost thousands of jobs.

stories.headline: Havana slams new Trump sanctions as ‘collective punishment’ of Cuban people
stories.summary: The Cuban government has firmly rejected the recent sanctions levied by US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Why markets are surging in spite of war
stories.summary: Even amid a supply shock, tech stocks continue to drive indices higher. Is the optimism justified?

stories.headline: Is China decoupling on food?
stories.summary: If this is pursued with the same energy as industrial policy, it will upend the agricultural economy

stories.headline: Berkshire Hathaway’s Cash Surges in Abel’s First Quarter as CEO
stories.summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc.’s cash pile soared to its highest level ever and operating earnings jumped in Greg Abel’s first quarter as chief executive officer.

stories.headline: Iran Juggles Oil Cuts and Storage Strain to Resist US Blockade
stories.summary: As the US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz tightens around Iran’s oil trade, exports have plunged in recent weeks and storage is rapidly filling. Already, the country has begun curbing production, according to a senior Iranian official.

stories.headline: Mace, Boebert and Luna Want Congress Members to Pay a Price for Sexual Abuse. Will It Work?
stories.summary: Three Republican women who had a hand in forcing the resignations of lawmakers accused of sexual misconduct said they are out to name and shame more offenders. It is not clear how far they will go.

stories.headline: Record-breaking May warmth soon to blow away as cold front moves towards eastern Australia
stories.summary: Daytime temperatures on Friday were 10 to 14C above average in four statesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastRecord-breaking warm temperatures for the start of May in many parts of the country will be washed out by a cold front bringing rain, thunderstorms and much cooler weather.A high-pressure system dragged warm northerly winds across south-east Australia, the Bureau of Meteorology (BoM) said. stories.headline: The KOSPI's miracle surge comes with a warning label
OPEC+ Provisionally Agrees June Quota Increase, Delegate Says
stories.summary: Major OPEC+ nations came to a provisional agreement on another modest but symbolic increase to supply targets for June, in the group’s first move since the UAE’s surprise exit, a delegate said.

stories.headline: Corporate America Earnings Beat Back Wall Street’s Wall of Worry
stories.summary: First-quarter earnings season is delivering Wall Street better-than-expected results, propelling US equities’ run from one record to the next.

stories.headline: New Rules for India Shadow Banks May Put Tata Sons IPO on Radar
stories.summary: A tweak by India’s central bank to the definition of shadow lenders has potentially revived the debate about whether Tata Sons Pvt. — the controller of one of the country’s largest conglomerates — could be forced to list in the near future.

stories.headline: Reinvention at the Fed and in Las Vegas
stories.summary: The FOMC held its meeting this week.

stories.headline: Oil Tanker Pricing Feud Embroils a Centuries-Old London Market
stories.summary: A legal claim from one of the world’s largest oil traders is throwing a spotlight on an arcane but critical corner of global finance — the multibillion-dollar freight market and the 282-year-old City of London institution that sits at the heart of it.

stories.headline: OPEC+ Agrees in Principle on Small Output Hike, Reuters Reports
stories.summary: Seven members of OPEC+ have reached an agreement in principle to raise oil output targets by around 188,000 barrels per day in June, Reuters reported Saturday, citing unnamed sources.

stories.headline: Finland Must Continue to Consolidate Public Finances, Rehn Says
stories.summary: Finland can no longer avoid or delay dealing with its growing public debt, Bank of Finland Governor Olli Rehn said.

stories.headline: Odd Lots: Inside a Booming Market for Dinosaur Fossils (Podcast)
stories.summary: Two years ago, Citadel’s Ken Griffin paid almost $45 million or a stegosaurus skeleton, making it the most expensive fossil ever sold at auction. So why are dinosaur bones joining the collections of millionaires instead of museums? How does the private market for fossils actually work? stories.summary: Wilderness Society says changes undermined intent of national standards intended to reverse decline of plants, animals and ecosystemsGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastGreen groups have accused the Albanese government of watering down a proposal to protect threatened species and ecosystems.National environmental standards were the key plank of reforms to Australia’s nature laws, passed by the parliament in November. stories.headline: Rallies under way as workers gather for International Labour Day
stories.summary: Workers, activists and trade unions around the world marked May Day with rallies on Friday.

stories.headline: Nepal celebrates return of stolen 13th-century Buddha statue from New York
stories.summary: The Himalayan nation restores centuries-old statue, stolen in the 1980s, to its original temple in capital Kathmandu.

stories.headline: Takaichi's musical diplomacy with Indonesia shows fresh bid for Asia partners
KKR bullish on sports investment, Asian stadium deals: co-CEO
Scandal-hit 'baijiu' maker sharply revises earnings with chairman missing
Trump Tells Congress Why He Doesn’t Need Its Authorization for the Iran War
stories.summary: In letters to the House and Senate, the president asserted that the hostilities had “terminated,” in an apparent attempt to avoid having to seek congressional approval.

stories.headline: A deadline for the Iran war is here. What does the War Powers Act say?
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asserted an ongoing ceasefire “pauses” the clock on the 60-day deadline to end hostilities in Iran or seek congressional approval.

stories.headline: Trump says he will hike tariffs on EU cars to 25%
stories.summary: The current level of tariffs charged on goods entering the US from the EU is 15%, under a deal negotiated last July.

stories.headline: It's not just oil: Iran war also threatens Asia's food security
stories.summary: Planting season is beginning in Asia - but the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Chinese restrictions has hit fertiliser supply.

stories.headline: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
stories.summary: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

stories.headline: Israel releases all but two activists in Greece after intercepting Gaza aid flotilla
stories.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla denounced the interception as "piracy", while Israel's foreign ministry called the flotilla a "PR stunt".

stories.headline: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says
stories.summary: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
stories.summary: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

stories.headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
stories.summary: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

stories.headline: US imposes sanctions on DR Congo ex-President Kabila alleging rebel support
stories.summary: The former leader has not responded to US accusations that he is aiming to destabilise the government.

stories.headline: Aung San Suu Kyi: The Myanmar democracy icon detained for years
stories.summary: Little has been heard of Myanmar's former leader since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

stories.headline: Trump says he will raise tariff on EU vehicles to 25%
stories.summary: Levies on cars and trucks exported to US will be imposed ‘next week’ unless companies manufacture at American plants

stories.headline: Fact-Checking Claims on Political Violence After Latest Trump Assassination Attempt
stories.summary: Republican commentators have argued, incorrectly, that political violence is largely a left-wing problem. Liberals have countered by citing the threats made against former President Obama.

stories.headline: Trump Threatens Higher Tariffs on European Cars
stories.summary: President Trump said that the European Union was not upholding its part of a trade agreement and that he would increase tariffs next week.

stories.headline: China scraps tariffs for all but one African nation
stories.summary: The zero-tariff regime gives China's soft power a boost, but may lead to uneven gains, say analysts.

stories.headline: Could Santa Marta climate talks mark ground zero in push to ditch fossil fuels?

stories.headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
stories.summary: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

stories.headline: Venice Biennale jury resigns days before start of top art exhibition
stories.summary: It follows growing tensions over the return of Russia for the first time since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

stories.headline: Hegseth argues Iran ceasefire ‘pauses’ deadline for Congress’s approval
stories.summary: The War Powers Act requires that the Trump administration seek lawmakers’ consent to continue hostilities beyond 60 days, a time limit that expires Friday.

stories.headline: Belgium plans to nationalise nuclear power plants
stories.summary: Prime Minister Bart De Wever said the move would see "less dependence on fossil fuel imports and more control over our own supply".

stories.headline: Ukraine expands oil strikes on Russia as Putin proposes brief ceasefire
stories.summary: In a phone call with President Trump, Putin proposed a ceasefire on 9 May, the anniversary of the defeat of Nazi Germany in WW2.

stories.headline: Wada investigation finds 300 Russian doping cases
stories.summary: More than 300 sanctions have been handed to Russian athletes following the seizure of Moscow laboratory data in 2019, the World Anti-Doping Agency says.

stories.headline: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
stories.summary: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.

stories.headline: Senators ban themselves from participating in prediction markets
stories.summary: The platforms, which have gained scrutiny recently, allow people to place bets on future events such as election results. The ban also applies to Senate staffers.

stories.headline: China to ban drone sales in Beijing citing security concerns
stories.summary: Drone users will also be required to register their devices and secure permits before flying them.

stories.headline: Elon Musk and Sam Altman bring their rivalry to court
stories.summary: Of the two unloved billionaires, which will the jury trust?

stories.headline: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
stories.summary: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

stories.headline: Hungary's next PM says frozen EU funds will be paid out soon
stories.summary: Péter Magyar meets EU leaders in Brussels, for the first time since his Tisza party won a landslide election.

stories.headline: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
stories.summary: The UAE's decision, after nearly 60 years of membership, is seen as a potential death knell for the oil cartel.

stories.headline: Faisal Islam: Why the UAE's exit from Opec is a big deal
stories.summary: It will have little effect on the current oil blockades, but it could change everything afterwards.

stories.headline: US soldier accused of betting on Maduro's removal pleads not guilty to fraud charges
stories.summary: The Army officer has been accused of using classified information to win $400,000 on an online prediction market.

stories.headline: Indian billionaire's son offers to save Escobar's hippos
stories.summary: Anant Ambani - son of Asia's richest man Mukesh Ambani - says he can take care of the animals roaming free since the drug lord's death.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.
